Add 5 classical cipher simulators: Caesar, Vigenere, Playfair, ADFGVX, Jefferson Wheel

Caesar: Shift cipher with slider, alphabet visualization, brute-force panel.
Vigenere: Polyalphabetic with keyword, Tabula Recta toggle.
Playfair: 5x5 grid digraph cipher with keyword-based grid generation.
ADFGVX: WWI fractionation + columnar transposition, step-by-step display.

Museum now has 16 simulators spanning 2000+ years of cryptography.

Fix: mobile session reset now properly archives transcript and refreshes Chat Page

- Fixed resetSession() to use accountId (pairingId) instead of from (mobileDeviceId)
  so the correct session key is resolved
- Transcript files are named by sessionId (UUID), not sessionKey — fixed archive
  to read the old sessionId from sessions.json before renaming
- Update sessions.json with new sessionId, reset token counts (same as gateway
  sessions.reset) so the Chat Page sees the session was reset
- Added real-time notification chain: plugin broadcasts gateway event →
  Desktop RPC client forwards as SSE → Chat Page clears messages immediately

commit.message:
Add cluster support and top_n filtering to get_storage_efficiency

Fixes:
- Added cluster parameter to get_storage_efficiency tool
- Added top_n parameter to limit results (prevents 1MB payload overflow)
- Sort volumes by savings (highest first) across all clusters
- Default to top 50 volumes when querying all clusters
- Calculate per-volume savings percentage
- Include cluster name in results

Features:
- Query specific cluster: get_storage_efficiency(cluster="gdccl1")
- Query all clusters: get_storage_efficiency() - returns top 50 by savings
- Get top 10 across all clusters: get_storage_efficiency(top_n=10)
- Filter by SVM: get_storage_efficiency(cluster="mhcl2", svm="svm1")

Behavior:
- Specific cluster + no top_n: returns all volumes from that cluster
- All clusters + no top_n: returns top 50 volumes (prevent payload overflow)
- All clusters + top_n: returns top N volumes
- Results always sorted by savings_bytes (descending)

This fixes the issue where querying all clusters returned too much data
and hit the 1MB MCP payload limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add editable schedule days (skip/recalculate) with unsaved-changes UX

Add skipDay() and recalculateFromDay() to scheduler.js for removing
individual days from a generated schedule. Users can skip a day (remove
as-is) or recalculate (regenerate from that point using prior entries as
history). UI shows a popover on each row's delete button, tracks unsaved
changes with a pulsing indicator on Save to Drive, and adds tooltips to
all Drive/CSV buttons. Includes 17 new CLI tests covering both functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restructure README for accessibility without losing substance

Reorder from implementation-first to problem-first: The Problem
(three compounding failures), The Approach (three-element methodology
with falsification principle), Getting Started, then Reference
(all technical detail grouped under one heading). Feature list moves
from introduction to reference section. Methodology and Dual-System
Protocol content integrated into The Approach. Horizontal rules mark
the boundary between conceptual overview and implementation detail.

Same content, better sequencing — a non-expert can follow the logic
before hitting pgvector dimensions and JSONB schemas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
add end-to-end envelope-before-block gossip pipeline test

Add integration test that exercises the full production-critical timing
race: envelope arrives via gossip before its block → buffered by
verify_payload_envelope_for_gossip → block imported via gossip →
process_pending_envelope drains buffer → payload_revealed=true.

Unlike the existing test which manually inserts into the buffer, this
test sends the envelope through process_gossip_execution_payload,
proving the verification function correctly buffers on BlockRootUnknown.

Also fix misleading log message: "Ignoring" → "Buffered" to accurately
reflect that the envelope IS stored for later processing.

commit.message:
feat: Workflow directory discovery + channel assignment state [Midtown !2138] (#1861)

* feat: add workflow directory discovery and ProjectPaths::workflows_dir()

Add WorkflowInfo struct and discover_workflows() to scan
~/.midtown/projects/<project>/workflows/<name>/ for directories
containing workflow.py. Optionally detects AGENTS.md per workflow.
Results are sorted by name for deterministic ordering.

Add workflows_dir() method to ProjectPaths for consistent path
construction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add channel-to-workflow assignment in daemon state + RPC

Add `channel_workflows: HashMap<String, String>` to
DaemonPersistentState for storing channel→workflow name mappings.
Multiple channels can share the same workflow.

Add three RPC methods:
- `workflow.assign` — assigns a workflow to a channel
- `workflow.unassign` — removes a channel's workflow assignment
- `workflow.list` — lists available workflows and current assignments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address review feedback — docs, directory comment, debug logs

1. Update docs/architecture.md: document all 5 workflow RPC methods
   (was "Two RPC methods", now includes workflow.assign/unassign/list)
2. Add workflows/ directory to paths.rs directory structure comment
3. Add channel_workflows count to load_for_repo/save_for_repo debug logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

FT2: sub-sample DT + DT-aware caller queue to reduce QRM 'over'

Decoder (ft2_decode.f90):
- Sub-sample DT interpolation via 3-point parabolic fit after fine sync
  search: DT accuracy improved from ±0.75ms to ±0.1ms
- EMA averaging now uses fractional ibest (best_xibest_avg) for
  more accurate multi-period DT tracking

Caller queue (mainwindow):
- enqueueCaller now accepts and stores DT for each caller
- FT2 hard filter: reject callers with DT > +1.0s or DT < -0.3s
  (outside guard time → guaranteed QRM in next period)
- Priority score = SNR - 8×|DT|: callers with precise timing
  served first, bad-timing callers pushed to end of queue
- Queue display now shows DT for each caller, borderline DT (>0.7s)
  highlighted in orange

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 5 bugs found during code audit

1. syncI2CBuffer out-of-bounds: prevent sequence[seqLen] access when
   first byte matches 0xFF, and re-check mismatched bytes as potential
   new sequence starts (both HALs)

2. irqCallback thread safety: make callback pointer std::atomic to
   prevent TOCTOU race between detachInterrupt() and IRQ thread

3. Signal handler async safety: replace std::cout with write() in
   term() so it is safe to call from signal context

4. Double cleanup prevention: null i2cBridgeHal after term() in
   PortduinoGlue.cpp cleanup handler

5. Use-after-free in recovery: null RadioLibHAL before deleting
   old HAL to prevent stale pointer access during 3s sleep

All fixes tested on device: meshtasticd starts clean (buffer sync OK),
IRQ polling works, clean shutdown shows message exactly once.

* fix: improve search relevance for short and multi-word queries

- Short query terms (<=2 chars like "ai") now use prefix/exact matching
  instead of substring LIKE, preventing "ai" from matching "trait", "email"
- search_symbols now also matches file paths, so symbols in relevant
  directories rank higher
- Ranking improved: name match (-10) > path match (-5) > signature (-3)
- Variables/consts deprioritized in rankings (usually noise)
- compress_context TF-IDF: short terms require exact token match, not
  substring, reducing false positives in scoring

with questions rather than declarations, derive from known invariants
rather than assert conclusions, and let the reader follow the logic
rather than being told the answer. Transcendent phenomenology presented
as structural questions the framework makes precise, not predictions.
Matches the epistemic register of the introduction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Profitability overhaul: fix churn, broken round-trips, and strategy weights

After 8 paper trading days the bot was down $745 (-0.75%) due to several
compounding issues. This commit addresses all 7 root causes:

Phase 0 — Emergency fixes:
- Widen take profit to +2% tiers / 33% sells (was +1% / 50%) and stop loss
  to -2% (was -1.5%) to fix negative expectancy churn loop
- Increase anti-churn cooldown to 120 cycles / 60 min (was 10 / 5 min)
- Add per-symbol daily trade limit (3 BUY fills/day max)
- Add startup DB reconciliation with Alpaca broker positions
- Reduce to 5 active strategies (deactivate sentiment, earnings, adaptive)

Phase 1 — Fix broken strategies:
- RSI mean reversion: revert thresholds to backtested 30/70 (was 35/65),
  add extreme oversold bypass (RSI < 25 skips 200-day MA filter)
- Add FIFO round-trip matching (_match_round_trip) so trade_results
  actually accumulates P&L records on SELL fills
- Call PerformanceTracker.update_all_weights() every 10 cycles so the
  arbitrator uses data-driven strategy weights instead of defaults

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
